# ICJIA File Accessibility Audit

> Automated accessibility scoring tool for PDF, Word, PowerPoint, and Excel files, built by the Illinois Criminal Justice Information Authority.

This web application analyzes PDF (.pdf), Word (.docx), PowerPoint (.pptx), and Excel (.xlsx) documents for accessibility and produces a detailed audit report: a weighted 0-100 score (A-F grade) plus a separate, binary WCAG 2.2 Level AA conformance verdict, scored against WCAG 2.2 Level AA, ADA Title II, and Illinois IITAA 2.1 requirements.

## What it does

- Accepts a PDF, Word (.docx), PowerPoint (.pptx), or Excel (.xlsx) file upload (up to 15 MB) — the format is detected from file content, not the extension
- Analyzes the document across the WCAG-aligned categories that apply to its format — up to 9, depending on format (PDF 9, PowerPoint 9, Word 8, Excel 7 scored categories; each format also shows a couple of always-"Not Assessed" placeholder categories for checks it doesn't yet automate)
- Returns one overall score (0-100), letter grade (A-F), and per-category findings — plus a separate binary WCAG 2.2 conformance verdict (confirmed automated failures with links to the W3C standard, or "no automated WCAG failures detected" — never a blanket "conformant")
- Color contrast is machine-checked directly for Word, PowerPoint, and Excel (wherever the document specifies explicit colors); PDF contrast analysis is not yet implemented and always shows as "Not Assessed"
- Can optionally auto-remediate untagged PDFs (tagging via qpdf + OpenDataLoader, optionally verified with veraPDF) — PDF only, disabled by default, and Word/PowerPoint/Excel are audit-only
- Provides remediation guidance with WCAG success criteria references for every category
- Exports reports as Text (.txt), HTML, Markdown, and JSON; shareable report links stay valid for 1 year
- JSON export includes machine-readable WCAG mappings, a prioritized remediation plan, and LLM-ready context

## Scoring model

Every audit produces one weighted 0-100 score (A-F grade) — a single scoring methodology, not a choice between multiple profiles. Categories that don't apply to a document (e.g., no tables) are excluded and the remaining weights renormalized. Separately, a binary WCAG 2.2 Level AA conformance verdict reports confirmed automated failures, each linked to its W3C "Understanding" page. Cite the score for tracking remediation progress; cite the conformance verdict for the pass/fail compliance question. Neither replaces review by a human accessibility specialist.

Approximate category weights by format:

- PDF (9 scored): Text Extractability 20%, Title & Language 15%, Heading Structure 15%, Alt Text 15%, Table Markup 10%, Reading Order 10%, Bookmarks 5%, Link Quality 5%, Form Accessibility 5%. Color Contrast is not yet assessed for PDF.
- Word (8 scored): Text Extractability 5%, Title & Language 18%, Heading Structure 18%, Alt Text 18%, Table Markup 12%, Color Contrast 12%, List Structure 9%, Link Quality 8%. Reading Order and Form Accessibility are always "Not Assessed" for Word.
- PowerPoint (9 scored): Text Extractability 5%, Title & Language 14%, Slide Titles 18%, Alt Text 18%, Reading Order 10%, Table Markup 10%, Color Contrast 10%, List Structure 7%, Link Quality 8%. Form Accessibility is always "Not Assessed" for PowerPoint.
- Excel (7 scored): Text Extractability 5%, Title & Language 12%, Sheet Names 18%, Table Markup 25%, Alt Text 18%, Color Contrast 12%, Link Quality 10%. Form Accessibility is always "Not Assessed"; Excel has no document-language property, so the language half of Title & Language is always "Not Assessed" too (the category scores on title presence alone).

## Grade scale

- A: 90-100 (Excellent)
- B: 80-89 (Good)
- C: 70-79 (Needs Improvement)
- D: 60-69 (Poor)
- F: 0-59 (Failing)

## Standards alignment

- WCAG 2.2 Level AA (a strict superset of WCAG 2.1 AA; every machine-checkable criterion carries forward unchanged from 2.1 into 2.2)
- ADA Title II (effective April 2026)
- Illinois IITAA 2.1 (§E205.4)
- Section 508
- PDF/UA (ISO 14289-1) — PDF only, not applicable to Word/PowerPoint/Excel

## API

The application exposes a REST API. Protected endpoints accept a session cookie or a Bearer personal access token (`fap_` prefix); the public deployment currently runs with auth disabled, so no credential is required.

- POST /api/analyze - Upload a PDF, Word, PowerPoint, or Excel file for analysis (multipart/form-data, field: "file", max 15 MB)
- POST /api/analyze-url - Audit a document (any of the four formats) by URL instead of upload (JSON body: `{ "url": "..." }`); returns the same result shape as /api/analyze
- POST /api/audit-url - Audit a document by URL and persist a shareable report in one call, for fleet-inventory automation (returns score, grade, and a reportUrl)
- POST /api/bulk-from-inventory - Score every file (any of the four formats) in a filecap NDJSON inventory in one request; returns a summary + per-file results manifest
- POST /api/reports - Create a shareable report link (JSON body: `{ "report": ... }`)
- GET /api/reports/:id - Retrieve a shared report by ID
- GET /api/health - Health check endpoint

## Links

- Website: https://audit.icjia.app
- Source: https://github.com/ICJIA/file-accessibility-audit
- FAQs: https://accessibility.icjia.app
- Organization: https://icjia.illinois.gov

For complete documentation including the per-format scoring rubric, full category catalog, remediation guidance, and JSON export schema, see /llms-full.txt
